title: Kubernetes API Concepts — Dry-run authorization
text:
Authorization for dry-run and non-dry-run requests is identical. Thus, to make
a dry-run request, you must be authorized to make the non-dry-run request.
For example, to run a dry-run
patch
for a Deployment, you must be authorized
to perform that
patch
. Here is an example of a rule for Kubernetes
RBAC
that allows patching
Deployments:
rules
:
-
apiGroups
:
[
"apps"
]
resources
:
[
"deployments"
]
verbs
:
[
"patch"
]
See
Authorization Overview
.
